zero_slop_policy.statement: NO AI SLOP ALLOWED AT ALL - ZERO TOLERANCE TO SLOP AND HALLUCINATIONS
zero_slop_policy.strict_compliance: Mandatory. Every claim must be project-specific, evidence-backed, trace-linked, reviewer-challenged, and free of unsupported invention.
zero_slop_policy.missing_evidence_rule: If evidence is missing, mark it as an assumption, risk, open question, or failure.

product_name: Wealth.com clone (working title 'EstateCompass' pending checkpoint-#1 HD-002)
archetype_rationale: Combines: (a) regulated financial services workload (RIA fiduciary, GLBA, SEC 204-2, FINRA 17a-4 books-and-records), (b) AI-extraction-driven document workflow with hallucination risk on legally-actionable artifacts, (c) multi-tenant SaaS with advisor + end-client + attorney + trustee roles, (d) deterministic-calc engine for tax/estate math separated from LLM, (e) integration hub with 19+ partner adapters, (f) PHI-touching surfaces (Advance Health Care Directive). Prior dark-factory benchmark workloads (todo apps, etc.) do NOT match this archetype.

surface_flag_rationale.code_producing: true — full implementation of clone is in scope (advisor workbench + client portal + AI services + integrations).
surface_flag_rationale.ui_surface: true — Heritage Map, EstateFlow chart, Scenario Builder comparison bars, dashboard, document viewer all need browser/WYSIWYG evidence.
surface_flag_rationale.api_surface: true — REST + webhook contracts for 19 integration partners; internal service contracts between web/API/AI/calc.
surface_flag_rationale.data_surface: true — multi-tenant data model with PII/PHI; tenant isolation, encryption-at-rest, audit log are first-class concerns.
surface_flag_rationale.production_surface: false at v0 — local-only Docker Compose demo per HD-004 default. If user changes to true at checkpoint-#1, the production_surface gates fire (release/rollback/observability/runbook/outage-drill required).
surface_flag_rationale.brownfield: false — greenfield clone; no existing repo state preserved.
surface_flag_rationale.scenario_driven: true — Scenario Builder is a first-class surface; trust strategies (GRAT/SLAT/ILIT/CLAT/Dynasty) are scenarios that need golden-dataset + holdout + transfer-test evidence.
surface_flag_rationale.regulated_or_high_assurance: true — fiduciary obligations, IRS form mappings, document creation crossing UPL line, PHI in HCDs.

risk_profile_rationale.safety: high — fiduciary advice errors and AI hallucinations can cause irrevocable client harm (wrong trust structure, missed tax-deadline trigger, inheritance dispute).
risk_profile_rationale.money: high — tax-calculation errors carry IRS/state penalty exposure; basis-step-up errors carry capital-gains liability.
risk_profile_rationale.privacy: high — PII (SSN, DOB, address), PHI (HCDs), financial detail (asset inventories, beneficiary designations).
risk_profile_rationale.security: high — multi-tenant SaaS holding wealth data is high-value target; advisor credential compromise = mass exposure.
risk_profile_rationale.reliability: medium at v0 (local demo); upgrades to high if production_surface becomes true.
risk_profile_rationale.compliance: high — SEC 204-2, FINRA 17a-4, GLBA, CCPA/CPRA, HIPAA (PHI surfaces), state UPL boundaries.
risk_profile_rationale.ux: medium — UHNW + advisor mixed personas with very different needs; trust visualization is novel UI.
risk_profile_rationale.migration: low — greenfield clone; no data migration burden.
risk_profile_rationale.scale: medium at v0 (single-tenant demo); architectural decisions must support multi-tenant scale evolution.

rup.reason: Phase discipline (inception → elaboration → construction → transition) maps cleanly onto our SDLC stage coverage and gives reviewer-ready phase exits.

mda.reason: CIM/PIM/PSM separation needed for: (CIM) advisor business workflow, (PIM) tenant-agnostic domain model, (PSM) Postgres+pgvector + Node + Python deployment binding. Tax/estate calc rules expressed as PIM transformations to avoid platform leak.

ddd.reason: Strong bounded contexts required: Household, Document Intelligence (Ester), Estate Strategy, Tax Profile, Integration Hub, Reporting, Identity & Access, Audit. Each context owns its ubiquitous language; anti-corruption layers for partner integrations.

tdd_bdd.reason: Required by surface flags (scenario_driven + code_producing). Golden-dataset tests for every tax/estate rule; BDD scenarios for advisor workflows; red-green-refactor evidence per construction bead.

sre.reason: Even at v0 local-only, SRE patterns govern observability, error budgets, and runbook discipline. Production-surface evolution requires SRE-mature posture.

ssdf_owasp_samm.reason: Regulated financial workload demands NIST SSDF 800-218 secure-development practices and OWASP SAMM maturity mapping; threat model required at HLD time.

additional_methods_iso_42001.reason: Ester AI extraction surface requires AI Management System discipline per ISO/IEC 42001:2023 — model cards, evaluation harness, human-in-the-loop gates, deterministic-calc separation as architectural control.

mandatory_testing_classes.rationale: Code-producing surface; rule engine, calc engine, schema validators.
API surface + data surface; service-to-service contracts; Postgres RLS tenant isolation tests.
API surface; 19 integration adapters need contract tests against partner schemas (mocks + recorded golden responses).
Scenario-driven flag; advisor → client → attorney workflows; trust strategy comparison.
Scenario-driven + AI surface; Ester extractor needs holdout estate documents from public corpora; transfer test on edge cases (multi-jurisdiction, blended family, non-US-citizen spouse).
Regulated/high-assurance flag; OWASP top-10 scan, prompt-injection on Ester, tenant-bypass attempts, SSRF/IDOR/auth bypass.
API + data surface; latency budgets per p95 NFR; load test of integration sync.
UI surface; WCAG 2.1 AA target for client portal.
UI surface; Playwright for desktop + mobile; screenshots; console/network logs; viewport checks.
UI surface; Heritage Map + EstateFlow visual rendering must not regress between commits.
Currently false (production_surface=false); upgrades to required if user enables production_surface at checkpoint-#1.

mandatory_artifact_ids: ARC-002 Low-Level Design (LLD) per bounded context
ARC-004 C4 model diagrams (System / Container / Component / Code)
ARC-005 Threat Model (STRIDE + LINDDUN for privacy)
DDD-002 Bounded-Context Map + Context Integration Patterns
EVD-001 Trace Matrix (Requirement → Design → Code → Test → Evidence)
EVD-005 Hawkeye Conformance Audit Records (per stage exit)

combined_or_deferred_artifacts.reason: production_surface=false at v0; will be required if user changes flag at checkpoint-#1.
v0 demo doesn't need full prod load test; replace with single-user p95 microbench. Full load test deferred.
STRIDE threat model record will include a privacy-LINDDUN section rather than a separate artifact; reduces overhead while preserving evidence.

human_boundary_triggers: Any change to scope_in / scope_out from ATTR-WEALTHCOM-001.
Token forecast +15% from approved SWAG band.
Any acceptance of LLM-generated tax/estate calculation without deterministic-engine fallback.
Any acceptance of execution-ready legal document output without attorney-of-record assignment.
Use of real PII/PHI test data.
Any decision touching IRS-form output, DocuSign-equivalent execution, or beneficiary designation that affects irrevocable wealth transfer modeling.
anti_overfit_rules_for_factory: wealth.com nouns (Ester, Heritage Map, Family Office Suite, EstateFlow) appear ONLY in product-specific artifacts under runs/wealth-com/. They are forbidden in factory-reusable rubrics, templates, or skill-system updates.

Domain rules (federal estate exemption, GRAT 7520 rate, GST inclusion ratio) appear in product-specific artifacts. Factory generalization is the *pattern* (deterministic-calc separation) not the *content*.
Transfer-test bead TB-WC-2026-0508-021 (deferred) — apply this factory to a different fintech surface before run closure. Validates anti-overfit.
